buggy_code:
import pandas as pd
import pytz

# Load market data with explicit timezone handling
df = pd.read_csv('ohlcv.csv', parse_dates=['timestamp'])

# Localize to UTC first, then convert to market timezone
df['timestamp'] = df['timestamp'].dt.tz_localize('UTC')
df['timestamp_et'] = df['timestamp'].dt.tz_convert('America/New_York')
df = df.set_index('timestamp')

# Filter to regular market hours only (9:30 AM - 4:00 PM ET)
market_open = pd.Timestamp('09:30', tz='America/New_York').time()
market_close = pd.Timestamp('16:00', tz='America/New_York').time()

df_market_hours = df[
    (df['timestamp_et'].dt.time >= market_open) &
    (df['timestamp_et'].dt.time < market_close)
]

print(f'Total rows: {len(df)}, Market hours rows: {len(df_market_hours)}')
clean_code:
import pandas as pd
import pytz

df = pd.read_csv('ohlcv.csv', parse_dates=['timestamp'])

df['timestamp'] = df['timestamp'].dt.tz_localize('UTC')
df['timestamp_et'] = df['timestamp'].dt.tz_convert('America/New_York')
df = df.set_index('timestamp')

market_open = pd.Timestamp('09:30', tz='America/New_York').time()
market_close = pd.Timestamp('16:00', tz='America/New_York').time()

df_market_hours = df[
    (df['timestamp_et'].dt.time >= market_open) &
    (df['timestamp_et'].dt.time < market_close)
]

print(f'Total rows: {len(df)}, Market hours rows: {len(df_market_hours)}')
description: Clean example — timezone-aware market data handling with explicit UTC localization and ET conversion.
explanation: Data is localized to UTC before any conversion. Conversion to America/New_York handles daylight saving time automatically. Market hours filter uses the converted timezone, not naive timestamps.
